name: remotepair
description: Turns the RemotePair IDE into a remote-pair client: live Remote Desktop of the host, input forwarding, one-click SSH connect, and host-side notifications.

commands.title: RemotePair: Set Up File Access (folder mapping / mount)
RemotePair: Reset Layout (terminal left, RD tab right)

walkthroughs.title: RemotePair setup
walkthroughs.description: Connect to your host, understand the host-side permission model, and install the AI extensions.

steps.title: Connect to your host
steps.description:
RemotePair drives a remote macOS host (default REMOTE_HOST from ~/.remote-pair/client.env). Click below to open the host filesystem over SSH.
[Connect to Host](command:remotepair.connectHost)

steps.title: Set up file access
steps.description:
Choose how this client reaches the host's files: Open Remote - SSH (edit the host directly), folder mapping + sync, or mount. Run the interactive setup to configure the host, folder mapping, and backend.
[Run setup](command:remotepair.setupFileAccess)

steps.title: Permissions are host-side
steps.description: Screen recording, accessibility (clicks/keys) and folder access are granted on the HOST machine (RemotePairHost.app), not on this client. If the Remote Desktop is blank, check the host's System Settings > Privacy & Security.

steps.title: AI agents run via the CLI
steps.description:
RemotePair runs AI agents (Claude, Codex, Gemini) via the terminal CLI — start one from the New Session picker. Only Open Remote - SSH is installed automatically (for host connections).
[Install Open Remote - SSH](command:remotepair.ensureExtensions)
